skip_reason: MANUAL ONLY — Infrastructure/security issue involving bot.js auth (isAllowed). Must be handled by owner via manual Claude request, not automated pipeline.
enhanced_description:
The Telegram bot for user "anshimitt" is failing to create GitHub issues when commands are sent.
- Affected component: bot.js or the GitHub issue creation pipeline
- Expected: When a user sends a bug/feature request via Telegram, a GitHub issue should be created automatically
- Current: Issues are not being created for this specific Telegram user
- Edge cases: Could be a username/chat_id mapping issue, API auth issue, or rate limiting
- Acceptance criteria: GitHub issues are successfully created when anshimitt sends requests via Telegram bot

title: smart reminder should be true by default
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

smart reminder should be true by default

---

**Category:** `enhancement`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description: User wants the smart reminder feature to be enabled (true) by default for new users or on first app load. Currently, smart reminders default to false/off, requiring the user to manually enable them. Affected component: the settings/defaults initialization logic in src/, likely where reminder preferences are set. Acceptance criteria: when a user first opens the app or creates a new profile, smart reminders are already toggled on; existing users who have explicitly set a value should not be affected.
implementation_notes:
Changed default value for smart reminders from false to true.
- Files changed: src/App.tsx
- Changed useState default for reminders from { feedInterval: 0, enabled: false } to { feedInterval: 0, enabled: true }
- Existing users who have saved a preference are unaffected since stored value overwrites default
- Only new users or users who never toggled the setting will see smart reminders enabled by default
- Type-check passes cleanly

title: welcome caurasel items I don’t want to be interactive, I want it to be animat...
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

welcome caurasel items I don’t want to be interactive, I want it to be animation on the behavior for both click and long press. Have the best experience

---

**Category:** `enhancement`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description: User wants the welcome carousel items to be non-interactive (no click or long-press handlers) and instead display smooth animations. Currently, carousel items may respond to tap/long-press with interactive behavior. The user wants the carousel to auto-animate or show animated transitions instead. Affected component: the welcome/onboarding carousel component in src/. Acceptance criteria: welcome carousel items no longer respond to click or long-press events; items display animations (e.g. auto-advance, fade, slide transitions) for the best onboarding experience.
implementation_notes:
Converted welcome carousel from interactive to auto-animated.
- Files changed: src/components/onboarding/WelcomeCarousel.tsx
- MiniDemo (Quick Log): Replaced interactive tap/long-press handlers with auto-play animation sequence that cycles through highlighting each button with toast notifications
- MomWellnessDemo: Replaced interactive meal/water/mood/vitamin/movement logging with auto-play sequence that progressively fills in wellness items with animated transitions
- MockBtn: Removed onClick, onTouchStart, onTouchEnd props — now purely display with animation support
- TapHint: Disabled (returns null) since carousel items are no longer interactive
- Removed swipe (onTouchStart/onTouchEnd) from main carousel area
- Made progress dots display-only (removed onClick navigation)
- Removed unused goTo, goPrev, touchX refs
- Continue button and Skip button still work for carousel navigation
- Type-check passes cleanly
